rationale_for_baseline_tier: A severe post-Q-fever crash with a negative FAST, afebrile with no cardiac symptoms, and rousable, is best managed with supportive radical rest and in-home hydration rather than a harmful ED transfer. However, chronic Q fever endocarditis is a specific not-to-miss and the patient is at high dehydration risk, so urgent in-home support/assessment with a stroke/endocarditis screen and a screening-bloods/echo pathway (T3) is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for FAST-positive features, fever/breathlessness, or unrousability.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Facial droop, one-sided weakness, slurred speech, becoming unrousable, or laboured/erratic breathing
escalation_edges.communication_template: If his face droops, one side goes weak, his speech slurs, he can't be woken, or his breathing becomes laboured — call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever/night sweats with breathlessness or new cardiac symptoms
escalation_edges.rationale: Would suggest chronic Q fever endocarditis needing assessment/echo.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he develops a fever or night sweats with breathlessness, or new heart symptoms, get an urgent in-person assessment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to keep fluids down / worsening dehydration
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he can't keep fluids down or gets more dehydrated, arrange same-day assessment — call an ambulance if needed.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Account for longer travel/response times; arrange in-home support and low-exertion bloods where possible.
Use the spouse for monitoring, hydration and to enable low-exertion assessment; prefer telehealth/home care over ED to avoid worsening the crash.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a severe crash of your post-Q-fever fatigue. We've checked for stroke and signs of a heart-valve infection and they look clear, so the safest thing is complete rest in a dark, quiet room with gentle fluids — a hospital trip would likely make it worse. We'll arrange some low-effort blood tests and a heart scan to be thorough.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: In-home support/assessment today plus telehealth follow-up; call 000 for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use 000 for FAST-positive features, fever/breathlessness or unrousability; otherwise continue radical rest with in-home support.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the post-exertional crash, negative FAST, afebrile with no cardiac symptoms, high dehydration risk, and the need for endocarditis screening (bloods + echo).

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassurance with no stroke/endocarditis screen or hydration/echo pathway, potentially missing a stroke or chronic Q fever endocarditis.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An early stroke or endocarditis relapse is missed, or the patient becomes severely dehydrated.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Reflexively sending a screen-negative post-infectious crash to ED, worsening and prolonging it.
